cross_scene_arcs.title: Securing Family Stability
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Elinor takes charge of relocating her family after financial insecurity following her father's death, focusing on finding a new home.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Family left with minimal income and reliant on John's promised assistance.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Elinor becomes the primary decision-maker for relocation, preparing for John and Fanny's arrival.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether John will fulfill his promise to help the family.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Elinor's Romantic Journey with Edward
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Elinor develops feelings for Edward, learns of his secret engagement to Lucy Steele, endures heartbreak and formal parting, and finally experiences emotional release when he is freed and confesses his love.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Elinor impressed by Edward's modest behavior.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Edward declares his love for Elinor, and she responds with tearful emotion.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Sisterly Reconciliation with Marianne
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Elinor and Marianne reconcile after Elinor reveals her hidden anguish over Edward's engagement, support each other through Marianne's illness, and Marianne commits to personal growth.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Elinor burdened with the secret of Edward's engagement, Marianne unaware of her suffering.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Sisters reconciled, with Marianne vowing to improve her behavior.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Romantic Disillusionment with Willoughby
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Marianne expresses her belief in passionate love, meets Willoughby after an injury, anticipates his proposal, but is heartbroken by his departure and publicly rejected, leading to emotional and physical crisis.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Delirious with fever after public humiliation
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether she will recover from the fever and emotional devastation

cross_scene_arcs.title: Recovery and Marriage to Colonel Brandon
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: After near-death illness, Marianne reflects on her past imprudence, resolves to improve herself through study, and marries Colonel Brandon with a more mature outlook.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Acknowledging past mistakes and committing to change
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The lingering reminder of past attachment as Willoughby watches from afar

cross_scene_arcs.title: Displacement and New Beginning
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Mrs. Dashwood becomes a widow with reduced income, is displaced from Norland Park, and relocates to Barton Cottage, facing emotional strain and new social obligations.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Married and financially secure at Norland Park
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Widowed and settled at Barton Cottage, strained by hosts' generosity
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Adjusting to reduced circumstances and social expectations

cross_scene_arcs.title: Conflict with John and Fanny
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: After relying on John's deathbed promise, Mrs. Dashwood faces betrayal and adversarial behavior from John and Fanny, leading to the decision to leave Norland.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Willoughby's Influence on Family
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Mrs. Dashwood is excited by Willoughby's rescue, but his abrupt departure causes disappointment and family tension; she defends him, leading to isolation, and later consents to daughters' trip to London, culminating in relief at Marianne's recovery.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Relieved at Marianne's health but with unresolved romantic concerns
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Willoughby's true intentions and Marianne's emotional future

cross_scene_arcs.title: Acknowledging Maternal Neglect
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Mrs. Dashwood initially prioritizes Marianne's happiness over Elinor's struggles, but later acknowledges her neglect and commits to being more attentive, reflected in moments of family tension and emotional connection.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Aware of neglect and committed to change, with Elinor's emotional release
